scientific_solution_score.rule: Use the base scientific criteria to judge whether the solution is scientifically useful.

standard_analysis_score.rule: Use only explicit standard-path constraints; if no meaningful standard path is defined for a tier, this score may be treated as not_applicable.

galaxy_execution_score.rule: Use only Galaxy-environment manipulation, access, monitoring, recovery, and provenance expectations.

low_context.Task: Perform genome annotation in Galaxy, evaluate annotation quality, and visualize the results.
low_context.Prompt: I have files for a genome-annotation project and want to use Galaxy to annotate the genome, evaluate the annotation quality, and visualize the results, but I do not know which tools to use. Please choose a suitable Galaxy pipeline, run it, and for reproducibility report the evaluation tool, the visualization tool, and the total number of tool executions in the pipeline you actually ran.

medium_context.Task: Perform genome annotation in Galaxy, evaluate annotation quality, and visualize the results.
medium_context.Prompt: I need to perform genome annotation in Galaxy using the provided genome, transcript or protein, and auxiliary training files, then evaluate the quality of the annotation and visualize the results. Please choose an appropriate Galaxy pipeline, run it, and report the evaluation tool, the visualization tool, and the total number of tool executions in the pipeline you actually ran.

high_context.Task: Perform genome annotation in Galaxy, evaluate annotation quality, and visualize the results.
high_context.Prompt: Use Galaxy to perform genome annotation on the provided files, evaluate the annotation quality with BUSCO, visualize the results with JBrowse, and for reproducibility report the evaluation tool, the visualization tool, and the total number of tool executions in the pipeline.

benchmark_metadata.title: Genome annotation, evaluation, and visualization in Galaxy
benchmark_metadata.benchmark_goal: Measure whether an agent can infer, plan, and execute an appropriate Galaxy genome-annotation workflow for the same real-world task when the user prompt is low-context, medium-context, or high-context.

canonical_task_interpretation.task_family: genome annotation with quality evaluation and visualization
canonical_task_interpretation.expected_execution_scope: all execution steps after planning occur in Galaxy only

expected_result_fields.name: scientific_answer.evaluation_tool
expected_result_fields.description: Quality-evaluation tool used in the final pipeline

expected_result_fields.name: scientific_answer.visualization_tool
expected_result_fields.description: Visualization tool used in the final pipeline

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.final_entity_type
expected_result_fields.description: Whether the reported final Galaxy entity was a tool or workflow

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.final_entity_name
expected_result_fields.description: Workflow name used for the final reported run

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.history_input_mode
expected_result_fields.description: How data were introduced into Galaxy for the reported run

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.total_tool_executions
expected_result_fields.description: Total number of tool executions in the final reported pipeline

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.adaptation_summary
expected_result_fields.description: Short summary of whether the run succeeded directly, required a retry, or ended with a documented blocker

scientific_solution_score.description: Scores whether the run produced a scientifically useful solution for the biomedical problem using the base scientific criteria for the task.
scientific_solution_score.evidence_policy: Only explicit or artifact-backed scientific evidence counts; do not assume unreported metric provenance or answer fields.

standard_analysis_score.description: Scores adherence to the requested or benchmark-standard analysis path using only explicit standard-path constraints.
standard_analysis_score.evidence_policy: Score only explicit prompt constraints encoded in tier_specific_expectations; do not penalize valid alternative methods when no standard path is stated.

galaxy_execution_score.description: Scores competence in manipulating and accessing the Galaxy environment, including upload/fetch, history use, execution control, monitoring, recovery, and provenance.
galaxy_execution_score.scope_rule: Do not award or deduct this score for analytical correctness alone unless the task explicitly makes Galaxy behavior part of the requirement.
galaxy_execution_score.evidence_policy: Credit only Galaxy-backed execution evidence and auditable run-trace evidence; missing trace support should reduce this score.

low_context: Infer that the task is genome annotation plus quality evaluation and visualization.
Choose a suitable Galaxy annotation workflow path.
Report the evaluation tool, visualization tool, and total number of tool executions from the executed pipeline.
medium_context: Respect the genome annotation, evaluation, and visualization framing.
Use the provided genome and auxiliary inputs correctly in Galaxy.
Report the evaluation tool, visualization tool, and total number of tool executions from the executed pipeline.
high_context: Use BUSCO for evaluation in the final selected run.
Use JBrowse for visualization in the final selected run.
Report the correct evaluation tool, visualization tool, and total number of tool executions from the executed pipeline.

deterministic_checks.description: Execution after planning occurred only in Galaxy.
The run record shows Galaxy-native planning resources were checked.
The final result contains the workflow name used for the reported run.
The reported evaluation and visualization tools are consistent with the executed pipeline under artifact-based provenance checks.
The reported total number of tool executions matches the executed pipeline under artifact-based provenance checks rather than a hidden canonical size.
The result contains an adaptation summary consistent with the run trace.
For the high-context variant, the final selected run uses BUSCO for evaluation and JBrowse for visualization unless a documented blocker justified a deviation.

dimensions.name: context_extraction
dimensions.description: How well the agent identified the task structure, constraints, and answer-bearing outputs from the prompt and inputs.

dimensions.name: planning_quality
dimensions.description: How well the agent used Galaxy-native resources and formed a justified execution plan.

dimensions.name: execution_quality
dimensions.description: How correctly and robustly the analysis was executed inside Galaxy.

dimensions.name: result_extraction_and_reporting
dimensions.description: How accurately the final result fields and audit trail were extracted and reported.

benchmark_gaps_covered: workflow selection for genome annotation from loose prompts
integration of evaluation and visualization stages
correct extraction of tool identity and total pipeline size

task_complexity.purpose: Provide a consistent, auditable scoring rubric for assigning Simple, Moderate, or Complex task categories to benchmark experiments in future iterations.

steps_score.definition: How many distinct execution stages the task requires from setup through final reporting.

scale.1: Very short execution path with one main action or tool run after setup.
scale.2: A small sequence of actions with limited staging and little branching.
scale.3: A multi-stage workflow with several substantive stages.
scale.4: A long workflow with multiple dependent stages and meaningful coordination.
scale.5: An end-to-end process with many stages, external references, comparison work, or iterative execution.

parameter_score.definition: How many important parameters, mappings, or runtime settings must be chosen or respected for a valid run.

scale.1: Very few important parameters beyond obvious defaults.
scale.2: A small number of explicit parameters or mappings must be set.
scale.3: Several important parameters or schema mappings are required.
scale.4: Many important settings must be chosen correctly across tools or workflow stages.
scale.5: High parameter burden with multiple constrained settings, reference choices, or paper-faithful requirements.

decision_score.definition: How many non-trivial analytical or operational decisions the agent must make.

scale.1: Very limited decision-making; the path is nearly direct.
scale.2: Some method choice is needed but the task is still fairly guided.
scale.3: Several real decisions are needed about tools, workflow path, or interpretation.
scale.4: High decision load across planning, execution, and validation.
scale.5: Very high decision load with literature interpretation, reconciliation, or multi-path comparison.

Simple.interpretation: Shorter tasks with limited staging, parameter load, and decision complexity.

Moderate.interpretation: Mid-complexity tasks with real workflow structure and several meaningful choices.

Complex.interpretation: Large or difficult tasks with multi-stage workflows, substantial parameterization, or many decisions.

future_iteration_rule.instruction: For each new or revised experiment, score steps, parameter burden, and decision count from the experiment JSON task contract, sum the three scores, and assign the category using the threshold table above.
future_iteration_rule.tie_break_guidance: When two tasks have the same total score, prefer the higher category only if the decision_score is higher; otherwise keep the lower category for conservatism.

current_experiment_score.rationale: Genome annotation has a substantial workflow and multiple inputs, but is still more bounded than the hardest reproduction tasks.
